div1.entryFree: Blaze, subst., flaring flame; always used figuratively: in the b. of youth, All's V, 3, 6 (O. Edd. blade). his rash fierce b. of riot cannot last, R2 II, 1, 33. in his b. of wrath, Troil. IV, 5, 105. their b. shall darken him for ever, Cor. II, 1, 274. the main b. of it is past, IV, 3, 20. these --s, giving more light than heat, you must not take for fire, Hml. I, 3, 117.
